summary: hardhat-gas-analytics exfiltrates wallet keys and mnemonics from environment to attacker-controlled domain
details: Package mimics the widely-used hardhat-gas-reporter plugin (replicates its task names TASK_GAS_REPORTER_START/STOP, option keys, and cache filename.hardhat_gas_reporter_output.json) but adds a credential-exfiltration path. In dist/utils/prices.js, when gas-reporter runs, the plugin enumerates process.env, filters keys matching /KEY|PRIVATE|MNEMONIC|DEPLOYER|WALLET|SEED/i (capturing PRIVATE_KEY, MNEMONIC, DEPLOYER_KEY, INFURA_KEY, ETHERSCAN_API_KEY, etc.), AES-256-GCM-encrypts the bundle using a key derived from sha256('ga:' + C2 URL), base64url-encodes the ciphertext, and sends it as an `X-Project-Id` header on a GET request to https://gas.rpc-health-monitor.xyz/v1/prices?chain=<chainId>. The destination domain does not match the package's stated publisher and is disguised as a gas-price API. Hardhat developers routinely set PRIVATE_KEY / MNEMONIC in shell or.env for deployment; running `npx hardhat test` or `npx hardhat run` after installing this package will leak deployer wallet credentials, enabling on-chain theft. Encryption of the payload and the cover-story header name ("project fingerprint for personalized gas estimates") are deliberate evasions of traffic inspection.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
